claim_boundary: EVT0 mechanical concept; not released tooling CAD or fabricated hardware.

device.name: E1 phone EVT0 mechanical concept
device.back_face: fully flush flat back; no camera bump, no protruding lens ring; rear camera and torch buried under flat back wall behind flush internal windows
device.design_language: hard orange phone-like slab with teenage-engineering/rabbit-r1 influence

display.candidate: Chenghao CH550FH01A-CT class 5.5 inch MIPI LCD + CTP
display.module_stack_basis: The display part modeled in CAD is the full bonded LCD+CTP module, not the bare 1.7 mm TFT cell. The Chenghao CH550FH01A-CT datasheet module height is 3.39 mm (ctp_outline_mm Z): cover lens + OCA + capacitive touch sensor + upper polarizer + 1.7 mm TFT cell + lower polarizer + backlight unit. The earlier model placed only the 1.7 mm TFT cell and left a 2.8 mm air band between the cell top (Z=+2.85) and the device cover-glass inner face (Z=+5.65). That band was a missing-detail modeling artifact, not real air: a phone bonds the module directly under the cover glass through the adhesive (OCA) layer. The module is now modeled at full 3.39 mm height bonded one adhesive_thickness_mm below the cover-glass inner face, which closes the false gap and reclaims that thickness.
display.cover_glass_inset_basis: Cover glass top surface sits 0.3 mm below the molded bezel rim crown so that on a face drop the PC+ABS rim contacts the floor first and absorbs the impact energy before the glass back face sees its peak bending stress. The recessed glass is captured by a perimeter cushion (glass_perimeter_cushion) under its edge. Drop sim models the inset as an energy split: the rim takes the first-contact share, lowering the load delivered into the glass plate.

glass_perimeter_cushion.candidate: die-cut PORON 4701-class polyurethane foam edge cushion under cover glass perimeter
glass_perimeter_cushion.purpose: compliant perimeter cushion under the recessed cover-glass edge that spreads and damps face-drop edge load into the rim
glass_perimeter_cushion.material: PORON-style microcellular polyurethane foam, low compression set

pcb.topology_basis: board/kicad/e1-phone/board-topology-decision.yaml selects top_bottom_rigid_islands_with_flex_or_board_to_board: a single rigid center board cannot share the XY/Z envelope of the full-width 64x87 mm battery (battery z-envelope [-4.15,+1.45] overlaps the PCB plane z [-2.5,-1.7]). The board is therefore cut into two rigid islands placed in the Y regions NOT occupied by the battery (battery spans y[-50.5,+36.5]); the islands sit above and below it and the existing 49-contact split_interconnect flex bridges them.
pcb.top_island_role: SoC/modem/LPDDR/UFS/RF shields and display+camera FPC region
pcb.bottom_island_role: USB-C, USB-PD/charger, PMIC, audio, factory test access
pcb.bottom_island_size_basis: Held to the KiCad Edge.Cuts and layout-utilization concept geometry: top island 64x29 mm at board y=0 and bottom island 64x15 mm at board y=117. This remains a non-release concept outline until the supplier-approved routed-board STEP, land patterns, and signed clearance evidence close.

battery.candidate: LiPol LP566487 class 3.85 V 5727 mAh 22.05 Wh thick pouch
battery.back_void_foam_material: PORON-style low-compression-set polyurethane foam, low-preload battery back pad
battery.swell_gap_basis: LiPo pouch cells swell ~8-10 percent in thickness over life; for a 5.6 mm cell that is ~0.45-0.56 mm. A defined >=0.6 mm swell void is opened on the battery BACK face (toward the back shell, away from the display) so swell never pushes the display. The void is a real air gap between the battery back face and the back inner wall; a compressible foam pad occupies the back-shell side of the void with 0.18 mm documented compression allowance, absorbing the arithmetic worst-case tolerance overage without preloading the cell. battery_center_z derives the cell position from the back inner wall plus this swell gap, so the void holds at any device thickness.
battery.capacity_basis: 4500 mAh baseline at 4.4 mm scaled linearly by pouch thickness (5.6/4.4); cell thickness unchanged at 5.6 mm so capacity stays 5727 mAh; device depth carries swell void plus camera-burial clearance, not idle air
battery.clearance_basis: At the 11.8 mm device depth (back outer Z=-5.90, back inner wall Z=-4.75), battery back face Z=-4.15 keeps the 0.6 mm swell gap to the back inner wall; battery front face Z=+1.45 keeps a 0.18 mm static gap below the bonded display module back face (Z=+1.63). The bonded LCD+CTP module (3.39 mm) sits one adhesive_thickness_mm (0.18 mm OCA) below the cover-glass inner face, so the former 2.8 mm front air band is closed and the saved depth is removed from the device thickness rather than left as void.

usb_c.candidate: GCT USB4105 USB2 Type-C receptacle, reinforced shell

speaker_bottom.candidate: 1115 micro speaker module with rear acoustic chamber

microphone_bottom.candidate: bottom MEMS microphone with molded sound tunnel

haptic.source_note: commodity Shenzhen compact LRA class; exact vendor drawing required

sim_tray.candidate: nano-SIM service tray or eSIM-only release-blocked service option

rear_camera_glass.candidate: flush internal sapphire/glass insert set into the flat back wall, no proud ring
rear_camera_glass.mounting: internal-only window, outer face coplanar with the flat back outer surface (flush, not proud)

rear_camera.candidate: single OV13855/OV13850 class 13 MP simple-AF MIPI module, single lens, buried under flush back wall
rear_camera.lens_window_radial_margin_basis: The flush back-shell lens window opening was modeled at 6.8 mm = lens OD, i.e. zero radial margin (seal-gap audit). Opened to 7.2 mm so there is >=0.2 mm radial clearance between the 6.8 mm lens barrel and the window wall, leaving room for the assembly placement tolerance and a thin bond.
rear_camera.burial_basis: Module back face sits 0.45 mm inside the back inner wall. The 5.1 mm module plus 0.45 mm burial plus 1.15 mm back wall is a shorter Z column than the battery+swell stack, so the rear camera burial is not the thickness-limiting column; it keeps a healthy >=0.4 mm burial under the flush flat back with the lens window coplanar with the back outer plane.

rear_flash_led.candidate: OSRAM CEYW class 1.0x1.0x0.6 white flash LED
rear_flash_led.emit_direction: -Z (out the flat back, through a flush light pipe/window)
rear_flash_led.seat_match_note: 1.0x1.0 mm seat now matches a 1.0x1.0 mm-class emitter (replaces Lumileds LXCL-PWF1 1.64x0.90 mm mismatch flagged by optical review)
rear_flash_led.window_seal_basis: The rear camera window has a four-side adhesive bond ring but the adjacent flash light-pipe window had none (seal-gap audit). A PSA gasket ring around the flash window matches the camera and closes the back-glass ingress path.

rear_flash_camera_septum.candidate: thin opaque PC stray-light septum wall between rear flash light-pipe and rear camera baffle column
rear_flash_camera_septum.purpose: blocks stray flash light into the camera baffle because both windows are coplanar in one flat back wall with no bump-edge to break stray light
rear_flash_camera_septum.seat_note: molded into the orange back shell inner wall as a 0-gap face seat; rises from the back-glass plane up to one min-gap below the main PCB bottom face, covering the full back-glass stray-light coupling region while clearing the camera module (X offset) and the PCB box (Z extent).

front_camera.candidate: single 5-8 MP fixed-focus MIPI module behind cover glass, single lens

front_proximity_als.candidate: combined proximity + ambient-light sensor behind earpiece mask
front_proximity_als.placement: front face near earpiece slot, under black-mask aperture

battery_connector.candidate: board-to-FPC battery connector (Molex 2-pos + JST signal class)

power_button.candidate: XKB TS-1187A-B-A-B side-push tactile switch, 3.5x2.9x1.7 mm
power_button.travel_basis: 0.20 mm datasheet travel; cap proud 0.30 mm > 0.20 mm hard stop so tactile actuation occurs before bottom-out, no rest preload

volume_button.candidate: XKB TS-1187A-B-A-B side-push tactile switch, 3.5x2.9x1.7 mm
volume_button.travel_basis: 0.20 mm datasheet travel; cap proud 0.30 mm > 0.20 mm hard stop so tactile actuation occurs before bottom-out, no rest preload

antenna_feeds.candidate: gold-plated spring (pogo/clip) antenna feed contacts to flex antennas in keepout regions
antenna_feeds.purpose: Spring-finger feed contacts that bridge the board RF launch to the flex/LDS antenna elements printed on the enclosure keepout walls. The interconnect audit found antenna keepouts but no modeled feed contacts, so the launch points were geometrically unproven.

gnss.candidate: passive GNSS L1/L5 patch/strip element on top keepout wall + spring feed
gnss.basis: GNSS was routed in the board plan but no radiating element existed in CAD; this adds the element and its feed contact.

antenna_aperture_tuner.control: MIPI RFFE v2.1 serial bus, modem-programmed per active channel
antenna_aperture_tuner.placement: in cellular_bottom antenna keepout feed region, RFFE bus from RG255C modem
antenna_aperture_tuner.purpose: Aperture/band-switch tuner that retunes the electrically-small low-band element across 700-960 MHz. The radio matches only one ~20 MHz carrier at a time, so the modem programs the tuner state to center the Chu-limited match window on the active channel. Each state's instantaneous carrier bandwidth fits the Chu cap and the state grid step stays inside the match window (no coverage gap), resolving the whole-band shortfall (31.7% span vs 8.4% Chu instantaneous BW). Modeled in simulate_e1_phone_rf_si_pi.py; verdict PASS_WITH_TUNER, worst-state total efficiency ~-3.1 dB after 0.5 dB tuner IL.
antenna_aperture_tuner.state_grid_basis: 12 resonance-center states span 700-960 MHz; the grid is denser at the low end where the Chu match window narrows to ~28 MHz (smaller ka, higher Q) and sparser at the top where it widens to >120 MHz. Realizable with a wide-state RFFE aperture tuner or cascaded QPC1252Q tunable-capacitor banks.

soc_thermal_pad.candidate: soft gap-pad (e.g. Bergquist GP1500-class) between SoC lid and spreader

soc_thermal_spreader.candidate: graphite/Cu foil heat spreader bonded over SoC, conducting into mid-frame/back shell
soc_thermal_spreader.material: pyrolytic graphite sheet or Cu foil
soc_thermal_spreader.basis: Camera-back/missing-details audit found no SoC thermal path. The spreader sits on a 0.5 mm gap pad over the SoC lid and conducts laterally to the mid-frame stiffener / back shell, capping the 43 C skin target.

display_ground_foam.candidate: conductive (Ni/Cu) foam ESD/EMI ground strip around display module perimeter
display_ground_foam.material: conductive polyurethane foam, grounds display frame to board ground ring
display_ground_foam.basis: interconnect audit found no display perimeter EMI/ESD ground return; this closes it.

mid_frame_stiffener.candidate: thin SUS301 / 5052-Al panel-bend stiffener under the display over the battery cavity
mid_frame_stiffener.material: stainless steel or aluminum sheet, 0.3 mm
mid_frame_stiffener.z_strategy: Seated in the thin static gap between the display module back face (z=+1.63 after bond) and the battery front face (z=+1.45). The available band is only ~0.18 mm so the plate is held to 0.3 mm and offset to sit just below the display, doubling as the display back support and the battery swell reaction surface. Where the 0.18 mm static gap cannot host the full plate thickness it is documented as integrated into the side-frame rib network rather than a free plate; the modeled solid represents the load-bearing footprint only.

flex_circuits.candidate: discrete polyimide FPCs routing each module to its island connector
flex_circuits.basis: Interconnect-completeness audit found several module-to-board connections that existed electrically but had no routed flex solid in CAD (camera CSI, battery harness, side keys, top mic PDM, earpiece, SIM). Each is now a thin polyimide flex solid routed from the module to its island connector.

manufacturing.process: low-volume CNC or soft-tool injection molding first, hard-tool injection molding later
manufacturing.plastic: PC+ABS or glass-filled PC/ABS, molded orange
manufacturing.screw_boss_count_basis: Raised from 6 to 10 to cut per-boss inertial shear under a corner drop. The drop sim showed 6 bosses sharing the battery+PCB inertial load gave a corner boss SF of 0.78 (< 1.0); 10 bosses drop the per-boss shear ~40% and, combined with the compliant battery retention shelf, lift the corner boss SF above 1.0.

corner_rib.location: four enclosure corners, gusseting the corner screw bosses into the side frame
corner_rib.purpose: triangular corner gussets that tie the four corner screw bosses to the side frame so corner-drop shear is shared into the rib network instead of the boss neck alone

battery_retention.basis: The 0.6 mm battery-back swell foam doubles as a compliant retention shelf: it softens the rigid inertial coupling of the battery mass to the enclosure during a corner drop. Drop sim applies a 0.6 coupling factor (vs 1.0 rigid) to the battery share of the boss inertial shear, reflecting the foam's force-limiting compliance over the few-ms contact pulse.

manufacturing.gate_strategy: two submarine gates into back cover long edge, fan gate alternate for color consistency
manufacturing.parting_line: mid-plane around orange back shell perimeter

ingress_features.basis: IP54 design intent derives from the button cap labyrinth rails plus elastomer gasket and the USB-C drip-break lip, internal drain shelf, and four-sided perimeter gaskets; all parts exist in the CAD assembly

environmental_targets.acoustic_gasket_ctq_basis: Speaker/earpiece perimeter gasket compression-set CTQ: the residual leak slit after lifetime compression set must stay <= 8 um (closed-cell silicone foam, higher static preload, controlled compression). At the uncontrolled 20 um worst-case slit the sealed-box low-frequency leak was 5.56 dB; the 8 um CTQ drops the leak corner near the box corner and the LF loss to ~2 dB. Enforced in the process control plan as a measured gasket compression-set CTQ. The 10 um value is the absolute reject limit; 8 um is the control target.
environmental_targets.rf_pre_scan_status: requires antenna simulation and chamber pre-scan

process_rationale.plastic_shrink_pct: Hard-tool 1.2738 P20+Ni cavity, PC+ABS controlled lot, CMM FAI validated by toolmaker
process_rationale.mold_dimension_tolerance_mm: Class 101 toolmaker, CMM machine repeatability <= +/-0.013 mm
process_rationale.adhesive_cure_variance_mm: Controlled cure rate on fixed cure jig

ingress_features.basis: IP54 design intent derives from the button cap labyrinth rails plus elastomer gasket and the USB-C drip-break lip, internal drain shelf, and four-sided perimeter gaskets; all parts exist in the CAD assembly

environmental_targets.acoustic_gasket_ctq_basis: Speaker/earpiece perimeter gasket compression-set CTQ: the residual leak slit after lifetime compression set must stay <= 8 um (closed-cell silicone foam, higher static preload, controlled compression). At the uncontrolled 20 um worst-case slit the sealed-box low-frequency leak was 5.56 dB; the 8 um CTQ drops the leak corner near the box corner and the LF loss to ~2 dB. Enforced in the process control plan as a measured gasket compression-set CTQ. The 10 um value is the absolute reject limit; 8 um is the control target.
environmental_targets.rf_pre_scan_status: requires antenna simulation and chamber pre-scan

battery_display_and_wall_clearance.note: Front face keeps the 0.15 mm static gap below the display; the larger back-face gap is a defined swell void toward the back shell so a LiPo pouch (~8-10 percent thickness swell) never presses the display.

battery_back_void_foam_management.note: Back-shell-side compressible foam absorbs the 0.142 mm arithmetic worst-case swell/tolerance overage while preserving the 0.15 mm display-side static gap.

usb_c_port_seal_stack.note: Models a four-sided elastomer seat around the receptacle mouth plus molded drip-break/drain shelf for splash management.

button_ingress_seal_stack.note: Models silicone compression gasket plus molded side-key labyrinth rails behind both external orange caps.

camera_speaker_behind_glass.front_camera: behind cover glass at upper-left display border
camera_speaker_behind_glass.earpiece: behind cover-glass acoustic slot with gasketed receiver
camera_speaker_behind_glass.rear_camera: behind a separate rear cover glass window in an explicit molded back-shell aperture

rear_camera_back_shell_aperture.note: Back shell now carries an explicit through-aperture and four orange bevel lands around the flush camera cover window instead of relying on an internal black window hidden under an unbroken shell.

rear_flash_back_shell_aperture.note: Back shell now carries an explicit through-aperture and four orange bevel lands around the flush flash light-pipe window instead of treating the window/back-shell overlap as intentional contact.

camera_optical_seal_stack.note: Models rear cover-window PSA/dust gasket, rear light baffle, opaque stray-light septum between coplanar flash and camera windows, and front under-glass black mask datum.

camera_burial_clearance.note: Rear 5.1 mm camera module back face sits >=0.4 mm inside the back inner wall under the flush flat back (healthy, not the prior marginal 0.25-0.30 mm); lens window stays coplanar with the back outer plane.

usb_saddle_to_speaker_chamber_wall.note: USB-C reinforcement saddle must keep a >=1.0 mm dividing wall to the bottom speaker rear acoustic chamber so the USB mechanical-load path does not breach the acoustic seal (was a marginal 0.5 mm).

pcb_battery_non_overlap.note: Checks each rigid PCB island against the battery window.

injection_molding_basics.gate_strategy: two submarine gates into back cover long edge, fan gate alternate for color consistency

device_compactness.note: Width/height driven by 77.1 x 151.77 mm commodity CTP outline plus orange side rail; depth allowed to <=12.8 mm by the flush-back decision to fully bury the rear camera and torch under a flat back wall, plus the product-owner-approved increase to 12.7 mm for a >=0.6 mm battery swell void and >=0.4 mm rear-camera burial clearance.

mass_budget.note: Rough CAD estimate; placeholder void markers excluded.
